title: Package Download Anomalies
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="artifactory:access"
| stats count by package_name, client_ip
| eventstats avg(count) as avg_downloads, stdev(count) as stdev_downloads by package_name
| where count > avg_downloads + 3*stdev_downloads
description: Unusual package download patterns may indicate dependency confusion attacks or compromised internal packages.
value: Unusual package download patterns may indicate dependency confusion attacks or compromised internal packages.
implementation: Monitor package download patterns. Baseline normal download volumes per package. Alert on statistical outliers. Watch for downloads of internal packages from external IPs. Track new/unknown packages being introduced.
visualization: Table (anomalous downloads), Bar chart (top downloaded packages), Line chart (download volume trend).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Artifactory/Nexus access logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Repository access logs (download events).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor package download patterns. Baseline normal download volumes per package. Alert on statistical outliers. Watch for downloads of internal packages from external IPs. Track new/unknown packages being introduced.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="artifactory:access"
| stats count by package_name, client_ip
| eventstats avg(count) as avg_downloads, stdev(count) as stdev_downloads by package_name
| where count > avg_downloads + 3*stdev_downloads
```

Understanding this SPL

**Package Download Anomalies** — Unusual package download patterns may indicate dependency confusion attacks or compromised internal packages.

Documented **Data sources**: Repository access logs (download events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Artifactory/Nexus access logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: artifactory:access. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="artifactory:access". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by package_name, client_ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by package_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > avg_downloads + 3*stdev_downloads` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the repository admin UI and download logs. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (anomalous downloads), Bar chart (top downloaded packages), Line chart (download volume trend).
grandmaExplanation: We watch packages and artifacts so supply-chain risk, storage, and policy checks stay under control.
knownFalsePositives: Alerts from scheduled scans, pilot repositories, or vendor API throttling during bulk operations.
